text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
I'm heading home from work when I<n>hear what
sounds like an argument.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Sirius, I'd appreciate it if you could<n>ease up a
little.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: Say what now?

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Time and time again, you have taken<n>a large
group of students under your<n>wing and occupied
the track, have<n>you not?

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
We've received several complaints<n>regarding
this matter. While I<n>understand that you have
your<n>reasons, that is no justification to<n>hinder
the other students' training.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
All right, let's say I give up guiding<n>them. How do
you plan on taking care<n>of them?

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
In that regard, I believe there is room<n>for
discussion.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
It is true that the students who come<n>to you are
indeed falling behind on<n>their training.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
To address that, I'm considering<n>hosting a
special intensive training<n>program, complete with
a dedicated<n>training ground and instructors.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Ah, yes. What a perfectly<n>by-the-numbers
answer. Typical.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Tell me. What happens to the people<n>who miss
out on this special<n>program?

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
In the event that happens, we shall<n>furnish yet
another opportunity.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
And what happens if they can't<n>attend that one
either?

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text: We would create yet another.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: Hah, a lovely plan! It just goes on<n>and on!

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
...Look here, O mighty Emperor. You<n>don't
understand a single thing.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Most, if not all, of us are born with<n>some sort of
issues we have to face.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Sickness. Injuries. Poverty. Poor<n>conditions
growing up.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
And those issues will rear their ugly<n>heads when
you least expect it,<n>whether that be during a race
or<n>training. We have no say in the<n>matter.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
What I'm saying is that, unlike a<n>model student
like you, they don't<n>have a guaranteed "next
time."

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
That means we have to focus on the<n>present. But
if you don't look at each<n>of them individually, you
can't see<n>the present.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
I see... You mean to say that it is in<n>our best
interests to employ a training<n>system that
addresses their individual<n>needs.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
There is a logic to your argument.<n>Unfortunately, it
is also not very<n>feasible.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
The result of bowing to every<n>individual need is
that everyone ends<n>up neglected. Not to mention,
we<n>cannot give special treatment to any<n>one
portion of the student body.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
To that end, I conclude that providing<n>equal
opportunities for everyone does<n>the greatest
good.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
The discussion has come to a<n>standstill, with
both parties at clear<n>odds. Just as I'm worrying
about<n>how to find an outcome...

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: ...Sigh. There's no point incontinuing this.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Hey, you there. I know you've been<n>secretly
listening in. Well, which one<n>of us is right?
choice_data_list:
I agree with Rudolf's idea of prioritizing the
collective.

I agree with Sirius's idea of prioritizing individual
runners.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Tracen Academy cannot function if<n>we lose sight
of what's best for<n>everyone. As a trainer, I have
to<n>agree with Symboli Rudolf.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: I see. So you're also on the side of<n>privilege.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: I hate when people talk down to me.<n>But...

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
It takes guts to look me in the eye<n>and shoot me
down. I can respect<n>that.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Fine. I'll take you all down next time.<n>That other
stick-in-the-mud included.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Perhaps pleased at having had the<n>opportunity to
speak her mind openly,<n>Sirius Symboli leaves
looking rather<n>satisfied.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: Sigh. And this is why I loathetalking to the privileged.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Must be nice just observing things<n>from up on
high.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
But don't forget, you never know<n>what people
will do when their backs<n>are to the wall.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
...Careful you don't find yourself on<n>the receiving
end of such indifference<n>one day.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
A uniform approach can't solve the<n>problems
individuals face. I agree<n>with Sirius Symboli that
we should<n>respect the point of view of each
student.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: Ah, so you can be reasoned with. Areyou one of us, by any chance?

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text:
Hey, O mighty Emperor. You're not<n>omnipotent.
You can't haggle with<n>those who don't fit your
mold, and<n>we'll never understand each other.
It's time you come to terms with<n>reality.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Even should that be the truth, I wish<n>to continue
my efforts in hopes that<n>we can come to a
mutual<n>understanding some day.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text: Naturally, that means with you as<n>well, Sirius.

text_block_list.name: Sirius Symboli
text_block_list.text: Hah. Is that so? If you really mean it,you could start by ditching thatpompous, self-aggrandizing monikerof yours, Emperor.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Their relationship is like oil and water,<n>but their
shared ideal is probably<n>closer than they think. If
they could<n>both give just a little bit, surely one
day they could get somewhere...
